title: Matter trace precheck
implementation_summary: Added deterministic metadata-only Permission And Audit Binding Matter trace precheck for RP00.P02.M05.S04.

The S04 policy, prechecker module, getter, validator assertions, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README entry, RP00 validator checks, and service tests bind a frozen Matter trace surface after RP00.P02.M05.S03 tenant boundary precheck.
The precheck accepts only a carried S03 permission_audit_binding tenant-boundary allow_metadata_only result with precheck_passed true, RP00.P02.M05 source, and RP00.P02.M05.S03 marker.
Matter-scoped metadata is allowed only when Matter id exists and Matter tenant remains tenant-aligned; firm-level metadata with no Matter context is allowed.
Matter-required claims without a Matter id and Matter tenant without a Matter id are denied with specific blocked.synthetic.permission_audit_binding.matter_trace refs.

The implementation rejects forbidden and unknown Matter trace claims fail-closed, including runtime permission, AuthZ evaluator, permission engine, security trimming, audit write, persistence, service execution, runtime route, human approval bypass, Claude review bypass, and source completion claims.

The result carries S01 service entrypoint contract refs, S02 request normalization ref, S03 tenant boundary ref, S14 Claude review required-routing refs, permission/audit/role/deny/security-trim refs, binding contexts, required evidence refs, acceptance gate IDs, and RP00.P00.M04.S01 Matter-first trace rules ref.

The allowed scope remains metadata-only and does not execute service logic, create runtime routes, evaluate runtime permissions, call AuthZ evaluators, run permission engines, apply security trimming, append audit ledgers, write audit events, write database, write storage, persist product state, mutate entity registries, or replace human approval.
Claude Opus 4.8 max read-only review returned safe_to_close with no P0, P1, or P2 findings. One informational advisory was adjudicated as non-blocking.
RP00.P02.M05 remains open and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S05 Permission precheck. RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
